title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Term Insurance - Is it an investment or expense?
Meta description: Term insurance may seem like an expense to most people because it is not a typical investment product. But what is the reality of investing in term insurance?
Keywords: term insurance calculator, term insurance benefits, online term plan
What is term insurance?
A term insurance policy is a kind of pure life cover that is relatively more affordable than other types of life insurance. When you purchase a term insurance plan, you give your family the benefit of a long-term financial safety net. In case something untoward happens to you, the life insurance provider pays out death benefits to your nominee. In return for this life cover, all you need to do is periodically pay your insurer the premiums as specified in the policy you purchased.
These premiums are lower than the premiums charged for other types of life insurance because a term plan offers death benefits only. You can bring down the costs further by opting for an online term plan.
What people think about investing in term insurance
There are many term insurance benefits that you get to experience when you buy a term plan. However, most people are not aware of these advantages. For first time buyers, term insurance plans may seem more like an expense than an investment. This is because the cover offered by a term plan is valid for a specified period. And if the policyholder passes away during this period, the insurer pays out death benefits to the nominee.
However, if the policyholder outlives the policy term, there are no maturity benefits paid out. So, here are some things that people generally think about investing in term life insurance.
If I outlive the policy term, I will not receive any financial benefits.
It seems very depressing to think of the possibility of premature death that may occur during the policy term.
Instead of paying premiums for years and never getting any financial benefits in return, I could invest in a fixed deposit or some other guaranteed income option.
The reality of investing in term insurance
The reality, however, is quite different. There are many term insurance benefits that people are unaware of. If you too have been wondering about whether a term plan is an investment or merely an expense, check out the many ways in which term insurance can actually be extremely helpful to you and your family.
It gives your family financial freedom
Although it may seem pessimistic to think about it, you never know what may happen in the future. Life is very uncertain, and in case something unexpected happens to you, your family may be financially stressed out in your absence. The payouts from a term insurance plan gives your family financial freedom in this case. You can make use of a term insurance calculator to figure out the amount of coverage needed to secure your family.
It helps protect your family’s assets
You may have taken on many debts to pay for assets like a house or a new car. In your absence, your family may not have the funds needed to pay off these debts. Fortunately, with the payouts from a term plan, you can be rest assured that your family can retain their assets without any financial burden. When you compare these benefits with the low cost of term plans, they turn out to be extremely cost-effective. Online term plans improve the cost-effectiveness further.
There are multiple tax benefits
Unlike many other investment options like fixed deposits or recurring deposits, the payouts from term insurance plans are tax-free. Furthermore, term insurance also gives you tax benefits under section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961, according to which the premiums paid are deductible from your taxable income. So, term insurance gives you tax benefits during the policy term and on the payouts.
You get added protection from riders
Beyond the basic coverage offered by term plans, there are also optional add-on riders that you can choose at the time of policy purchase. These riders give you financial benefits in case of a critical illness diagnosis, a surgery or hospitalization, or an accident that results in permanent partial or total disability.
Conclusion
All things considered, term insurance may not be an investment in the traditional sense of the word. But it surely gives you financial benefits that few other financial products can offer. And it secures the future of your family in any eventuality. That’s why term insurance should be an essential part of any financial portfolio.
https://www.canarahsbclife.com/blog/term-insurance/Is-term-insurance-an-investment-or-an-expense.html

Term Insurance - Is it an investment or expense? Meta description: Term insurance may seem like an expense to most people because it is not a typical investment product. But what is the reality of investing in term insurance? Keywords: term insurance calculator, term insurance benefits, online term plan
What is term insurance? A term insurance plan is a kind of pure life cover that is relatively more affordable than other types of life insurance. When you purchase a term insurance plan, you give your family the benefit of a long-term financial safety net. In case something untoward happens to you, the life insurance provider pays out death benefits to your nominee. In return for this life cover, all you need to do is periodically pay your insurer the premiums as specified in the policy you purchased. These premiums are lower than the premiums charged for other types of life insurance because a term plan offers death benefits only. You can bring down the costs further by opting for an online term plan. What people think about investing in term insurance There are many term insurance benefits that you get to experience when you buy a term plan. However, most people are not aware of these advantages. For first time buyers, term insurance plans may seem more like an expense than an investment. This is because the cover offered by a term plan is valid for a specified period. And only if the policyholder passes away during this period, the insurer pays out death benefits to the nominee. However, if the policyholder outlives the policy term, there are no maturity benefits paid out. So, here are some things that people generally think about investing in term life insurance.
● If I outlive the policy term, I will not receive any financial benefits. ● It seems very depressing to think of the possibility of premature death that may occur during the policy term. ● Instead of paying premiums for years and never getting any financial benefits in return, I could invest in a fixed deposit or some other guaranteed income option. The reality of investing in term insurance The reality, however, is quite different. There are many term insurance benefits that people are unaware of. If you too have been wondering about whether a term plan is an investment or merely an expense, check out the many ways in which term insurance can actually be extremely helpful to you and your family. 1. It gives your family financial freedom Although it may seem pessimistic to think about it, you never know what may happen in the future. Life is very uncertain, and in case something unexpected happens to you, your family may be financially stressed out in your absence. The payouts from a term insurance plan gives your family financial freedom in this case. You can make use of a term insurance calculator to figure out the amount of coverage needed to secure your family financially.
2. It helps protect your family’s assets You may have taken on many debts to pay for assets like a house or a new car. In your absence, your family may not have the funds needed to pay off these debts. Fortunately, with the payouts from a term plan, you can be rest assured that your family can retain their assets without any financial burden. When you compare these benefits with cost of term plans, they turn out to be extremely cost-effective. Online term plans improve the cost-effectiveness further.
3. There are multiple tax benefits Unlike many other investment options like fixed deposits or recurring deposits, the payouts from term insurance plans are tax-free under section 10(10D) of Income Tax Act, 1961. Furthermore, term insurance also gives you tax benefits under section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961, according to which the premiums paid are deductible from your taxable income. So, term insurance gives you tax benefits during the policy term and on the payouts, and these benefits are subject to the provisions stated in the Income Tax Act, 1961.
4. You get added protection from riders Beyond the basic coverage offered by term plans, there are also optional add-on riders that you can choose at the time of policy purchase at nominal extra charges. These riders give you financial benefits in case of a critical illness diagnosis, a surgery or hospitalization, or an accident that results in permanent partial or total disability. Conclusion All things considered, term insurance may not be an investment in the traditional sense of the word. But it surely gives you financial benefits that few other financial products can offer. And it secures the financial future of your family in case of an unforeseen eventuality. That’s why term insurance should be an essential part of any financial portfolio.
